content.text: I’ve got the main constraints now. The shared environment should include Emotion global styles, `LeafyGreenProvider`, the real toast provider, a router, and global MSW for `AuthProvider`’s mount-time auth query plus login/logout. From here I’m choosing story targets directly from existing tests so the JSX and interactions stay faithful to the codebase instead of becoming synthetic demos.

I’m at the point where the shape is clear enough to start implementation. Plan:
1. Add the shared Storybook runtime in `.storybook/preview.tsx`: Emotion globals, `LeafyGreenProvider`, real toast provider, deterministic date/cookies/storage seeding, and global MSW handlers for auth.
2. Update Storybook config for MSW/static assets and add the worker file so browser-mode stories can use the real fetch runtime.
3. Add colocated stories one file at a time from existing tests and app usage patterns, running `npx vitest --project storybook <story>` after each new file and fixing issues before moving on.
4. Finish with `npx tsc --noEmit` and one broader Storybook Vitest pass over the changed stories.

content.text: `IconWithTooltip` is turning out to be a LeafyGreen tooltip/runtime mismatch in Storybook’s browser harness rather than a useful application story target, so I’m dropping it for now instead of burning the whole turn on one flaky portal case. I’m continuing with components whose real tests already align better with Storybook browser execution.
